user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate 3‑phase PWM motor control to KIT_A2G_TC387_5V_TFT using GTM TOM1 with the unified IfxGtm_Pwm API.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate a center‑aligned 20 kHz two‑level 3‑phase inverter PWM on TOM1 with 6 complementary, active‑high outputs, 0.5 µs dead‑time, 1.0 µs minimum pulse, push‑pull pads (IfxPort_PadDriver_cmosAutomotiveSpeed1), TOM1 CH0 as master time base on cmuFxclk0 (GCLK). Map outputs to Port 02: U(HS=P02.0 CH1, LS=P02.7 CH2), V(HS=P02.1 CH3, LS=P02.4 CH4), W(HS=P02.2 CH5, LS=P02.5 CH6). Initialize duties U=25%, V=50%, W=75% and update duties synchronously. Duty ramping/transition profiles are implemented in the application layer; this PWM module provides synchronous duty update/latching only. Extension point: an application-supplied ramp generator invokes the module's update API to apply ramps.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified high‑level IfxGtm_Pwm driver to configure TOM1 with CH0 as the master time base sourced by cmuFxclk0 from GCLK, center‑aligned at 20 kHz. Enable GTM and CMU clocks via IfxGtm_enable and IfxGtm_Cmu_enableClocks. Configure TOM1 TOUT pins with IfxGtm_PinMap_setTomTout for push‑pull and pad driver settings. Provide complementary pairs and dead‑time through the unified driver configuration and, if needed, IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDeadTimeImmediate. Set initial duties (U=25%, V=50%, W=75%) using non‑immediate channel duty updates so latching occurs synchronously at the next timer update event.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high‑level GTM PWM driver supports multi‑channel synchronization, complementary pairs, dead‑time, and center‑aligned operation. It is available in the template and matches TC3xx. It simplifies migration from IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl while meeting the advanced PWM requirements.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3PhaseInverterPWM_updateDuties
runtime_function.purpose: Stage new U/V/W duties that will latch synchronously at the next PWM period boundary for all six complementary outputs.

testability.dependency_injection: Module accepts a HwIf ops table (enable, clock, pin_map, start/stop, duty/deadtime update); defaults bind to IfxGtm_/IfxPort_ implementations, and tests can inject mocks.

api_changes.reason: Unified high‑level API per user request
Unified high‑level API per user request
Unified API duty update; synchronized multi‑channel updates use non‑immediate calls that latch on the next period

migration_notes.context_preserved: TOM1 is used with CH0 as master time base (cmuFxclk0 @ GCLK), center‑aligned 20 kHz, complementary outputs with 0.5 µs dead‑time and 1.0 µs minimum pulse, active‑high, push‑pull pads (IfxPort_PadDriver_cmosAutomotiveSpeed1), and remapped to P02.0/2/1/4/5/7 as specified. Initial duties (U=25%, V=50%, W=75%) are applied using unified API synchronized shadow updates. Where reference used disable/applyUpdate, unified IfxGtm_Pwm uses non‑immediate update APIs to achieve the same synchronous latching at the period boundary.

header_selection.mapping_note: Use IfxGtm_enable to enable the GTM module; channel enablement is handled by IfxGtm_Pwm during initialization.
